results.title: Episcopal Church puts headquarters on market
results.description: The Episcopal Church, the spiritual home of many of America's founders, presidents and other members of the elite, announced Wednesday that it wants to sell or lease its 12-story New York headquarters, a sign of the shrinking of mainline Protestantism and institutional religion in the United States as well as the way religious groups are radically reshaping their commercial footprint in the 21st century.

results.title: Supreme Court Footpath Ruling Explained: Why It Matters for Every Indian City
results.description: In a historic verdict, the Supreme Court of India has ruled that the right to walk on a marked footpath is a fundamental right, and that walking rights should be preferred over driving. The court has also made it clear that safe and obstacle-free footpaths are a vital component of the right to life and movement guaranteed by the Constitution. This is considered one of the most important interventions into urban planning and pedestrian safety in recent times, owing to its emphasis on pedestrians over vehicular traffic. This could mean a lot for the millions of Indians who walk to [...]

Global Yoga Sessions Celebrate the 12th International Day of Yoga
results.description: As the 12th International Day of Yoga approaches, Indian diplomatic missions around the world are organizing yoga sessions to promote holistic health and well-being. With the theme 'Yoga for Healthy Ageing', events are taking place in countries like Kuwait, Finland, the Philippines, and the USA, showcasing the benefits of yoga across generations. From large gatherings in Kuwait to midnight yoga in the Arctic, these celebrations highlight the importance of yoga in fostering a balanced lifestyle. Join the global community in embracing the timeless practice of yoga for a healthier future.

results.title: Assam: Inmate Beaten to Death at Duliajan De-Addiction Centre
results.description: The alleged custodial death of a young inmate at a de-addiction centre in Assam ’s Duliajan has triggered shock and outrage across the region, raising questions about the functioning of rehabilitation facilities. The incident came to light at the Naoholia De-addiction-cum-Rehabilitation Centre, where a youth, identified as Aman Sonar, was allegedly brutally assaulted, leading to his death. Aman Sonar, son of Raju Sonar and a resident of Baruah Tiniali No. 15, Zaloni Grant in Duliajan, had been admitted to the centre for treatment. His body was recovered on Thursday (June 18), bearing multiple visible injury marks from head to toe, suggesting severe physical assault. According to allegations, the owner of the centre, Santosh Limbu of Margherita, along with staff members, repeatedly beat the victim through the night, ultimately leading to his death. The claims have intensified public anger, with demands for immediate accountability. In response, the district administration has sealed the rehabilitation centre, while the body has been sent to Assam Medical College and Hospital (AMCH) in Dibrugarh for post-mortem examination. The incident has sparked a strong reaction from local organisations. The Dibrugarh units and Duliajan regional committees of the Gorkha Students’ Union and Gorkha Mahila Parishad have submitted a memorandum to the Superintendent of Police, demanding swift arrests and strict punishment for those responsible. The organisations have issued a stern warning, stating that they will launch widespread protests if the accused are not identified and taken into custody within 24 hours. Also Read: Youth Found Dead at De-Addiction Centre in Nagaon, Probe Demanded
